serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of Nanyang Curry Seaweed Beancurd Slices Rice weighing approximately 400 g, comprising a mixed-grain rice base with curry sauce, seaweed, and beancurd slices.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from HPB Singapore Foodo: Curry Rice (vegetarian), Brown Rice, Seaweed, and Beancurd; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central for quinoa and seaweed components
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo) and USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Foodo entry exists for Nanyang Curry Seaweed Beancurd Slices Rice. Nutrient values estimated by weighted component analysis: mixed-grain rice base (red rice, brown rice, quinoa blend ~55% by weight), curry sauce (~20%), beancurd slices (~15%), seaweed (~5%), and condiments (~5%). HPB Foodo entries for curry rice, brown rice, and beancurd used as primary anchors. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used for quinoa (NDB 20137) and dried seaweed (NDB 11667) components. Values represent a plausible per-100g composite for this dish category.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed-grain rice and curry rice analogues; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Nanyang Curry Seaweed Beancurd Slices Rice. GI estimated at 55 based on analogous mixed-grain rice dishes (brown rice GI ~50, white rice GI ~72, quinoa GI ~53) weighted by approximate composition. The mixed-grain base (red rice, brown rice, quinoa) lowers the overall GI relative to plain white rice. Curry sauce and beancurd components have minimal glycemic contribution. Composite GI estimated at 55 (low category, ≤55). GL per serving = (55 × 20.5 × 4.0) / 100 = 45.1 g per 400 g serving. Source: University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 Diabetes Care international GI tables.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~1240 mg per serving) from curry sauce and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a plate and request reduced-salt curry sauce, as a full 400 g serving provides approximately 1240 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~45 per serving) despite low-GI mixed-grain base; large carbohydrate portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: The mixed-grain rice base (red rice, brown rice, quinoa) provides a lower GI (~55) than plain white rice, but the large serving size (400 g, ~82 g carbohydrate) results in a high glycemic load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce portion to 200–250 g and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Plant-based protein from beancurd, low saturated fat (~1.4 g per 100 g), and dietary fibre from mixed grains and seaweed
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is relatively cardioprotective due to its plant-based protein (beancurd), low saturated fat content, and fibre from mixed grains and seaweed; however, sodium from curry sauce should be monitored, and individuals with cardiovascular disease should prefer a reduced-sodium preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Nanyang Curry Seaweed Beancurd Slices Rice is a Singaporean fusion rice dish served at Flavours by Saute (Funan, Singapore). The dish features a mixed-grain rice base enriched with red rice, brown rice, and quinoa, which together provide higher dietary fibre, protein, and micronutrients compared to plain white rice. The rice is topped with a Nanyang-style curry sauce — a Southeast Asian curry influenced by Malay, Indian, and Chinese culinary traditions — along with sliced seaweed and beancurd (tofu) slices. The curry sauce is typically coconut-milk-based or spiced with lemongrass, turmeric, and chilli, giving the dish a rich, aromatic, mildly spicy flavour profile with a creamy texture. The beancurd slices contribute a soft, silken texture and plant-based protein, while the seaweed adds umami depth and trace minerals including iodine and calcium. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 148 kcal per 100 g, with 6.2 g protein, 4.8 g fat, and 20.5 g carbohydrate. The mixed-grain base lowers the overall glycemic index to approximately 55 (low GI), making it a more glycaemically favourable option than plain white rice dishes. Sodium content is moderate to high (~310 mg per 100 g) due to the curry sauce, which is a consideration for individuals managing hypertension. The dish is vegetarian and halal-suitable. It is priced at SGD 13.80 and represents a health-conscious, modern Singaporean café-style meal designed to balance flavour with nutritional value.

confidence_reason: No exact match found in HPB Foodo or any approved primary source for this specific dish. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted component analysis using HPB Foodo entries for curry rice, brown rice, and beancurd, supplemented by USDA FoodData Central for quinoa and seaweed. GI is an analogous estimate based on mixed-grain rice literature. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are approved and the estimation methodology is documented and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated composites; no single approved-source entry exists for this exact dish. Estimation based on HPB Foodo (curry rice, brown rice, beancurd) and USDA FoodData Central (quinoa NDB 20137, seaweed NDB 11667). 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA component sources used for this estimation. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo component sources. 5. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as this is a plant-based dish with no hydrogenated fats; confirmed negligible per USDA component data. 6. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero) as all protein sources are plant-based (beancurd, seaweed); no animal-derived ingredients identified. 7. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; naturally zero for this food type. 8. GI estimated at 55 (low, ≤55) based on analogous mixed-grain rice dishes from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. 9. Serving size estimated at 400 g based on typical Singaporean café rice plate portions. 10. Dietary flags: vegetarian status assumed based on dish description (beancurd, seaweed, curry); halal assumed based on plant-based ingredients and restaurant context; vegan status uncertain due to possible dairy in curry sauce. 11. Cuisine reclassified from 'Global' (input) to 'Singaporean' based on dish identity, restaurant location, and Nanyang culinary tradition.

Iteration 2: Verified GI category threshold (GI=55 is ≤55, correctly 'low'); confirmed all aliases_by_language entries present verbatim in flat aliases; confirmed zero-macro justification phrases present in notes for trans_fat, cholesterol, and vitamin_b12; confirmed all -1 sentinel values documented; confirmed ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact, closest_parent, none).
